title: Relate factors and multiples
description: Demonstrate understanding of factors and multiples.

assessment_items.question_content:
We know $9 \times 2 = 18$.

**So, which of the following statements are also true?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
When both factors are whole numbers, then:

$\blueD{\text{factor}} \times \blueD{\text{factor}} = \maroonD{\text{multiple}}$

$\blueD9 \times \blueD2 = \maroonD{18}$  

So, 

- $\blueD9$ and $\blueD2$ are $\blueD{\text{factors}}$ of $\maroonD{18}$.
- $\maroonD{18}$ is a $\maroonD{\text{multiple}}$ of $\blueD9$ and $\blueD2$.

The true statements are:

- $\blueD9$ is a factor of $\maroonD{18}$.


- $\maroonD{18}$ is a multiple of $\blueD2$.

- $\blueD7$ and $\blueD8$ are $\blueD{\text{factors}}$ of $\maroonD{56}$.
- $\maroonD{56}$ is a $\maroonD{\text{multiple}}$ of $\blueD7$ and $\blueD8$.

The true statements are:

- $\blueD7$ is a factor of $\maroonD{56}$.

- $\blueD8$ is a factor of $\maroonD{56}$.

assessment_items.question_content:
**What set of numbers are shaded on the hundred chart?**

![hundred chart with 1, 2, 3, 4, 6, 8, 12, and 24 shaded](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/02530f1e9ec0730d3178083137ff8ff48e4977df)

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
When both factors are whole numbers, then:

$\blueE{\text{factor}} \times \blueE{\text{factor}} = \maroonD{\text{multiple}}$

###Factors of $24$

$\begin{align}
\blueE{1} \times \blueE{24} &= \maroonD{24}\\\\
\blueE{2} \times \blueE{12} &= \maroonD{24}\\\\
\blueE{3} \times \blueE{8} &= \maroonD{24}\\\\
\blueE{4} \times \blueE{6} &= \maroonD{24}
\end{align}$

All of the shaded numbers in the table are factors of $\maroonD{24}$.
The shaded numbers on the hundred chart are only the factors of $24$.

assessment_items.question_content:
**What set of numbers are shaded on the hundred chart?**

![hundred chart with 1, 2, 3, 4, 6, 9, 12, 18, and 36 shaded](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/0ecb94e87861e137d107520de208afb754b5cc11)

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
When both factors are whole numbers, then:

$\blueE{\text{factor}} \times \blueE{\text{factor}} = \maroonD{\text{multiple}}$

###Factors of $36$

$\begin{align}
\blueE{1} \times \blueE{36} &= \maroonD{36}\\\\
\blueE{2} \times \blueE{18} &= \maroonD{36}\\\\
\blueE{3} \times \blueE{12} &= \maroonD{36}\\\\
\blueE{4} \times \blueE{9} &= \maroonD{36}\\\\
\blueE{6} \times \blueE{6} &= \maroonD{36}
\end{align}$

All of the shaded numbers in the table are factors of $\maroonD{36}$.
The shaded numbers on the hundred chart are only the factors of $36$.

assessment_items.question_content:
**What set of numbers are shaded on the hundred chart?**

[[☃ image 1]]

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
When both factors are whole numbers, then:

$\blueE{\text{factor}} \times \blueE{\text{factor}} = \maroonD{\text{multiple}}$

###Multiples of $6$

$\begin{align}
\blueE{6} \times \blueE{1} &= \maroonD{6}\\\\
\blueE{6} \times \blueE{2} &= \maroonD{12}\\\\
\blueE{6} \times \blueE{3} &= \maroonD{18}\\\\
\blueE{6} \times \blueE{4} &= \maroonD{24}\\\\
\blueE{6} \times \blueE{5} &= \maroonD{30}\\\\
\blueE{6} \times \blueE{6} &= \maroonD{36}\\\\
\blueE{6} \times \blueE{7} &= \maroonD{42}\\\\
\blueE{6} \times \blueE{8} &= \maroonD{48}\\\\
\blueE{6} \times \blueE{9} &= \maroonD{54}\\\\
\blueE{6} \times \blueE{10} &= \maroonD{60}\\\\
\blueE{6} \times \blueE{11} &= \maroonD{66}\\\\
\blueE{6} \times \blueE{12} &= \maroonD{72}\\\\
\blueE{6} \times \blueE{13} &= \maroonD{78}\\\\
\blueE{6} \times \blueE{14} &= \maroonD{84}\\\\
\blueE{6} \times \blueE{15} &= \maroonD{90}\\\\
\blueE{6} \times \blueE{16} &= \maroonD{96}
\end{align}$

All of the shaded numbers in the table are multiples of $\blueE{6}$. Every multiple of $6$ between $1$ and $100$ is shaded.
The shaded numbers on the hundred chart are only the multiples of $6$.

assessment_items.question_content:
Laura tried to shade all the multiples of $9$ on the hundred chart, but she made a mistake.

[[☃ image 1]]

**What mistake did Laura make?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
When both factors are whole numbers, then:

$\blueE{\text{factor}} \times \blueE{\text{factor}} = \maroonD{\text{multiple}}$

$\begin{align}
\blueE{9} \times \blueE{1} &= \maroonD{9}\\\\
\blueE{9} \times \blueE{2} &= \maroonD{18}\\\\
\blueE{9} \times \blueE{3} &= \maroonD{27}\\\\
\blueE{9} \times \blueE{4} &= \maroonD{36}
\end{align}$

So some multiples of $\blueE{9}$ are $\maroonD{9}$, $\maroonD{18}$, $\maroonD{27}$, and $\maroonD{36}$.

Laura only shaded one of those numbers, so she is not correct.
Laura shaded the factors instead of the multiples of $9$.

choices.content: Laura shaded the factors instead of the multiples of $9$.
Laura shaded $9$, but multiples are always greater than the number.
Laura shaded three multiples of $9$, but she left out some.

assessment_items.question_content:
Kieran tried to shade all the multiples of $3$ on the hundred chart, but he made a mistake.

[[☃ image 1]]

**What mistake did Kieran make?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
When both factors are whole numbers, then:

$\blueE{\text{factor}} \times \blueE{\text{factor}} = \maroonD{\text{multiple}}$

$\begin{align}
\blueE{3} \times \blueE{1} &= \maroonD{3}\\\\
\blueE{3} \times \blueE{2} &= \maroonD{6}\\\\
\blueE{3} \times \blueE{3} &= \maroonD{9}\\\\
\blueE{3} \times \blueE{4} &= \maroonD{12}\\\\
\blueE{3} \times \blueE{6} &= \maroonD{15}\\\\
\blueE{3} \times \blueE{8} &= \maroonD{18}
\end{align}$

So some multiples of $\blueE{3}$ are $\maroonD{3}$, $\maroonD{6}$, $\maroonD{9}$, $\maroonD{12}$, $\maroonD{15}$, and $\maroonD{18}$.

Kieran only shaded some of those numbers. So he is not correct.

[[☃ explanation 1]]

$\begin{align}
\blueE{9} \times \blueE{1} &= \maroonD{9}\\\\
\blueE{9} \times \blueE{2} &= \maroonD{18}\\\\
\blueE{9} \times \blueE{3} &= \maroonD{27}\\\\
\blueE{9} \times \blueE{4} &= \maroonD{36} \\\\
\blueE{9} \times \blueE{5} &= \maroonD{45}\\\\
\blueE{9} \times \blueE{6} &= \maroonD{54}\\\\
\blueE{9} \times \blueE{7} &= \maroonD{63}\\\\
\blueE{9} \times \blueE{8} &= \maroonD{72}\\\\
\blueE{9} \times \blueE{9} &= \maroonD{81} \\\\
\blueE{9} \times \blueE{10} &= \maroonD{90}\\\\
\blueE{9} \times \blueE{11} &= \maroonD{99}
\end{align}$

So the multiples of $\blueE{9}$ that are on a hundred chart are $\maroonD{9}$, $\maroonD{18}$, $\maroonD{27}$, $\maroonD{36}$, $\maroonD{45}$, $\maroonD{54}$, $\maroonD{63}$, $\maroonD{72}$, $\maroonD{81}$, $\maroonD{90}$, and $\maroonD{99}$.
Kieran shaded the multiples of $9$ instead of the multiples of $3$.

choices.content: Kieran shaded the factors instead of the multiples of $3$.
Kieran shaded the multiples of $6$ instead of the multiples of $3$.
Kieran shaded the multiples of $9$ instead of the multiples of $3$.

assessment_items.question_content:
Stef tried to shade all the multiples of $5$ on the hundred chart, but he made a mistake.

[[☃ image 1]]

**What mistake did Stef make?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
When both factors are whole numbers, then:

$\blueE{\text{factor}} \times \blueE{\text{factor}} = \maroonD{\text{multiple}}$

$\begin{align}
\blueE{5} \times \blueE{1} &= \maroonD{5}\\\\
\blueE{5} \times \blueE{2} &= \maroonD{10}\\\\
\blueE{5} \times \blueE{3} &= \maroonD{15}\\\\
\blueE{5} \times \blueE{4} &= \maroonD{20}
\end{align}$

So some multiples of $\blueE{5}$ are $\maroonD{5}$, $\maroonD{10}$, $\maroonD{15}$, and $\maroonD{20}$.

Stef forgot to shade $5$, so he is not correct.
Stef didn't shade $5$.

choices.content: Stef shaded the factors instead of the multiples of $5$.
Stef shaded the multiples of $10$ instead of the multiples of $5$.

assessment_items.question_content:
Francie tried to shade all the multiples of $8$ on the hundred chart, but she made a mistake.

[[☃ image 1]]

**What mistake did Francie make?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
When both factors are whole numbers, then:

$\blueE{\text{factor}} \times \blueE{\text{factor}} = \maroonD{\text{multiple}}$

$\begin{align}
\blueE{8} \times \blueE{1} &= \maroonD{8}\\\\
\blueE{8} \times \blueE{2} &= \maroonD{16}\\\\
\blueE{8} \times \blueE{3} &= \maroonD{24}\\\\
\blueE{8} \times \blueE{4} &= \maroonD{32}
\end{align}$

So some multiples of $\blueE{8}$ are $\maroonD{8}$, $\maroonD{16}$, $\maroonD{24}$, and $\maroonD{32}$.

Francie only shaded one of those numbers, so she is not correct.
Francie shaded the factors instead of the multiples of $8$.

choices.content: Francie shaded the factors instead of the multiples of $8$.
Francie shaded $8$, but multiples are always greater than the number.
Francie shaded the multiples of $4$ instead of the multiples of $8$.

assessment_items.question_content:
Carlos tried to shade all the factors of $30$ on the hundred chart, but he made a mistake.

[[☃ image 1]]

**What mistake did Carlos make?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
When both factors are whole numbers, then:

$\blueE{\text{factor}} \times \blueE{\text{factor}} = \maroonD{\text{multiple}}$

$\begin{align}
\blueE{1} \times \blueE{30} &= \maroonD{30}\\\\
\blueE{2} \times \blueE{15} &= \maroonD{30}\\\\
\blueE{3} \times \blueE{10} &= \maroonD{30}\\\\
\blueE{5} \times \blueE{6} &= \maroonD{30}
\end{align}$

So the factors of $\maroonD{30}$ are $\blueE{1}$, $\blueE{2}$, $\blueE{3}$, $\blueE{5}$, $\blueE{6}$, $\blueE{10}$, $\blueE{15}$, and $\blueE{30}$.

Carlos only shaded four of those numbers, so he is not correct.
Carlos shaded four factors of $30$, but he left out some.

choices.content: Carlos shaded the multiples instead of the factors of $30$.
Carlos shaded $15$, but $15$ is a multiple of $5$, so it cannot be a factor.
Carlos shaded four factors of $30$, but he left out some.

assessment_items.question_content:
Scarlett tried to shade all the factors of $15$ on the hundred chart, but she made a mistake.

![Hundred chart with 15, 30, 45, 60, 75, and 90 shaded.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/35a9225d4e945764681e7a38dc65aceb95be863a)

**What mistake did Scarlett make?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
When both factors are whole numbers, then:

$\blueE{\text{factor}} \times \blueE{\text{factor}} = \maroonD{\text{multiple}}$

$\begin{align}
\blueE{1} \times \blueE{15} &= \maroonD{15}\\\\
\blueE{3} \times \blueE{5} &= \maroonD{15}\\\\
\end{align}$

So the factors of $\maroonD{15}$ are $\blueE{1}$, $\blueE{3}$, $\blueE{5}$, and $\blueE{15}$.

Scarlett only shaded one of those numbers, so she is not correct.

$\begin{align}
\blueE{15} \times \blueE{1} &= \maroonD{15}\\\\
\blueE{15} \times \blueE{2} &= \maroonD{30}\\\\
\blueE{15} \times \blueE{3} &= \maroonD{45}\\\\
\blueE{15} \times \blueE{4} &= \maroonD{60}\\\\
\blueE{15} \times \blueE{5} &= \maroonD{75}\\\\
\blueE{15} \times \blueE{6} &= \maroonD{90}\\\\
\blueE{15} \times \blueE{7} &= \maroonD{105}
\end{align}$

So the multiples of $\blueD{15}$ that are on a hundred chart are $\maroonD{15}$, $\maroonD{30}$, $\maroonD{45}$, $\maroonD{60}$, $\maroonD{75}$, and $\maroonD{90}$.
Scarlett shaded the multiples instead of the factors of $15$.

choices.content: Scarlett shaded the multiples instead of the factors of $15$.
Scarlett shaded $15$, but factors are always less than the number.
Scarlett shaded the factors of $5$ instead of the factors of $15$.

assessment_items.question_content:
Elijah tried to shade all the factors of $50$ on the hundred chart, but he made a mistake.

![Hundred chart with 1, 5, and 25 shaded.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/2556500593a0881dcb55f106f8d4ca4fb1a33bfa)

**What mistake did Elijah make?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
When both factors are whole numbers, then:

$\blueE{\text{factor}} \times \blueE{\text{factor}} = \maroonD{\text{multiple}}$

$\begin{align}
\blueE{1} \times \blueE{50} &= \maroonD{50}\\\\
\blueE{2} \times \blueE{25} &= \maroonD{50}\\\\
\blueE{5} \times \blueE{10} &= \maroonD{50}
\end{align}$

So the factors of $\maroonD{50}$ are $\blueE{1}$, $\blueE{2}$, $\blueE{5}$, $\blueE{10}$, $\blueE{25}$, and $\blueE{50}$.

Elijah only shaded three of those numbers, so he is not correct.
Elijah only shaded the factors of $25$ instead of all of the factors of $50$.

choices.content: Elijah shaded the multiples instead of the factors of $50$.
Elijah shaded three numbers, but there are always an even number of factors.
Elijah only shaded the factors of $25$ instead of all of the factors of $50$.
